title: The Perennial Marshes

the epicenter of life as we had first known it to be
untouched by time, an amalgamation of freeze frames
creatures of every descent; animals, plants, fungi, bacteria
a sanctuary for [[Entity 10|hunters]], [[Entity 90|grazers]], [[Entity 28|scavengers]], [[The Teratoma|opportunists]]
flourishing with creatures so familiar yet so alien
...certainly rare around here, isn't it?
---- {{Class 0|color-h=63|color-s=25%|color-l=50%|safety=Varying Hazards|security=Sustained Ecosystem|entity=Lifeform Infestation|class=2|hexagon=❂}} ---- DISCOVERED: ~2600 B.C.E. // Moderate entry rate - Moderate exit rate // Unsuitable for permanent habitation ---- {{Image Banner|image=Muggymarsh.jpg}}
  ↟ '''OVERVIEW'''
{{Blurimage|width=350px|align=right|file=Rootswamp.jpg|caption=the scent isn't as sickening with you}} {{Blurimage|width=350px|align=right|file=Weirdcave.webp|caption=it's beginning to feel unnatural again}} '''The Perennial Marshes''' is a boundless landscape of flourishing wetlands encompassed by [[The Hive]], featuring a dense variety of natural biota that exhibit little to no anomalous characteristics. Although the level cannot be reliably inhabited by humans, it is regularly visited by nomadic wanderers and [[Entity 101|other sapient species]] as reliable means of hunting food. The environmental conditions of '''The Perennial Marshes''' are stable yet fluctuating with "seasons", overall maintaining a humid atmosphere and tolerably brisk temperatures. The ground, a dark soil-like substrate marbled with stone and peat, is damp with coursing water left behind from drizzling rainfall. Flat plains are a rarity in '''The Perennial Marshes;''' much of the landscape is rugged, scattered with mountainous ridges, flooded valleys, and roiled puddles. Separating the terrestrial surface is a branching web of lakes that split the ground into islands and extensive shores, ranging from shallow narrow channels to ocean-scale bodies of freshwater. These waters remain still for the most part — only being disturbed slightly by natural currents. The skies in '''The Perennial Marshes''' are persistently overcast, with crevasses of sunlight piercing from above during the warmer periods. A distant layer of cool-white fog obscures the horizon at all times. Drizzling rain is exceptionally common, with heavier downpours seldom occurring and providing a consistent source of hydration for various sessile species on the surface. Plant life in the level is present in nearly all environments, with mosses and mangrove trees dominating the earthy terrain. The natural surplus of nutrients and minerals within '''The Perennial Marshes'''' dirt supports the rapid growth and expansive coverage of vegetation. Plants and fungi in '''The Perennial Marshes''' serve as one of the lowest tiers of nutrition in the ecosystem's food chain, with species ranging from microbes to large grazing herbivores feeding from such sources. Other forms of basic nutrition include various types of non-living colonial mounds found in the ground (which, like viruses, are not technically considered "living" organisms). '''The Perennial Marshes''' supports a network of underground caverns accessible through crevices and tunnels that peer to the surface. These caves, majorly comprised of much of the level's natural stone and densely packed gravel, tend to increase in space the further they extend downwards. The structure of the caves is distinctly divided into narrow tunnels and open areas, with transitional areas between both types of spaces being abrupt and appearing unnatural. Some chasms of the level will provide passage to the lowermost point of the level, known as '''the Hollow;''' a continuous "chamber" floored with impenetrable stone. '''The Hollow''' is flooded with water at varying levels from chest-height to 30 meters deep. An abundance of pillars made from hardened soil are scattered throughout, forming a claustrophobic labyrinthine structure. There is almost no life to be found in '''the Hollow;''' only sparse numbers of scavenging worms and microscopic arthropods exist, feeding on cadavers that fall from the surface.
'''THE WILDBOUND PHENOMENON'''
Permeating '''The Perennial Marshes''' exists the '''Wildbound Phenomenon''' — the cause of biological lifeforms dominating the level as opposed to those with anomalous properties. The '''Wildbound Phenomenon''' refers to the effect of all entities with anomalous abilities gradually losing these properties permanently upon entering '''The Perennial Marshes'''. This phenomenon is supportive of the [[Entity Vessel Theory]], with the assumption that exposure to '''The Perennial Marshes''' can cause the rapid deterioration of an entity's psychodominous bond. In addition, all properties that could not reasonably exist within [[the Frontrooms]]' logic would vanish upon affliction by the '''Wildbound Phenomenon.''' Entities that only partially operate anomalously (such as [[Entity 26|Mercies]]' effects upon contact) will merely have their anomalous abilities revoked.
  ↟ '''NATURAL EVENTS'''
Much like the seasons of Earth in [[The Frontrooms|standard reality]], '''The Perennial Marshes''' possesses its own array of periodic natural events caused by the environment.
'''SEASONS'''
The seasons of '''The Perennial Marshes''' complete a cycle approximately every 892 days, with 3 individual seasons in each "year". Each season lasts an approximately equal amount of time. '''Season Mon''' is best likened to [[The Frontrooms|Earth's]] state of autumn, preceding '''Season Lae''' in '''The Perennial Marshes'''' seasonal cycle. Temperatures average 13°C (46°F), and traces of visible sunlight from '''Season Tel''' begin to vanish under the overcast skies. '''Season Lae''' is the coldest of all seasons within '''The Perennial Marshes,''' preceding '''Season Tel''' in '''The Perennial Marshes'''' seasonal cycle. Otherwise known as '''The Perennial Marshes'''' "deep winter", '''Season Lae''' averages temperatures of -4°C (25°F). Despite the environmental conditions supporting the formation of snow, no traces of such event has been found in '''The Perennial Marshes.''' Most species of the level thrive best in warmer temperatures; biological activity is likewise drastically decreased during '''Season Lae,''' with many lifeforms undergoing a form of hibernation or metabolic stasis at this time. '''Season Tel''' is the warmest of all seasons within '''The Perennial Marshes,''' preceding '''Season Mon''' in '''The Perennial Marshes'''' seasonal cycle. During '''Season Tel,''' the clouds begin to part and reveal sunlight from above. Due to UV burst activity, which will be explained later, temperatures during '''Season Tel''' fluctuate between 24°C (75°F) and 32°C (89°F). The surface is heavily illuminated during this time and allows for many leafy plants to flourish and acquire most of their energy. '''Season Tel''' is the period of mating for many animals and [[Kingdom Venatrexima|Venatrexens]].
'''UV BURSTS'''
UV bursts are random events in which large amounts of UV energy and [[Object 499|Kharthiact]] manifest in the deepest parts of '''The Perennial Marshes'''' caverns. UV bursts most commonly occur in '''Season Tel,''' yet can rarely happen in late '''Season Lae.''' It is unknown why exactly UV bursts occur in '''The Perennial Marshes;''' certain theories do, however, suggest a possible connection to [[Level 500]] as the source.
'''BASE RAIN'''
{{Blurimage|width=350px|align=right|file=Baseraining.jpg|caption=throw on your raincoat, quick!}} Base rains occur in '''The Perennial Marshes''' as a consequence of severe UV burst activity causing certain vapors to rise into the clouds. When a UV burst event occurs, the UV waves can disturb pockets of calcium bases lying within the soil of certain plains. This causes the bases to rise into the atmosphere when combined with the declining temperatures to '''Season Mon,''' causing a higher pH in following rainfall. Base rain serves as one of the biggest natural threats that can occur in '''The Perennial Marshes;''' likewise, various native lifeforms have developed unique methods of counteracting the hazardous exterior. Many aquatic species may simply migrate to larger bodies of water during the event, though terrestrial organisms face greater challenges. One of the most common strategies is to store a mobile food supply and take shelter in the caves — however, some species (including [[Kingdom Venatrexima|Venatrexens]]) may steal from others seeking refuge in these places, causing high levels of competition.
  ↟ '''LIFEFORMS'''
To create an absolute list of all life present in '''The Perennial Marshes''' would be infeasible due to its sheer diversity. For the sake of practicality, only the most distinct and/or notable species groups will be listed in the folder below:
3 file(s) found under "PERENNIAL_MARSHES", with 1 file(s) being accessible.
  ↳> [[The Perennial Marshes/Lifeforms|PERENNIAL_MARSHES_LIFEFORMS.pdf]]
  ↳> altesseron_kjgkfdbgk.dds (file does not exist)
  ↳> AEG_FEN_OPERATION.dds (file inaccessible)
  ↟ '''OUTPOSTS'''
Aurielle Agricultural - Station Paleno *Supply station founded by the [[Republic of Aurielle]]; *Consists of 192 officials and 0 members; *Station made to collect natural resources and use local wildlife as food sources; *No permanent residence for any members.
Location Venal *Supply station founded by Altesseron Company; *Consists of ~650 officials and 0 members; *Station made to harvest local wildlife for various products, including food, materials, and medicine; *Does not accept members.
  ↟ '''PASSAGES'''
'''ENTRANCES'''
* Portions of [[The Hive]] may transition into a muddy and rocky composition; following these paths will cause one to enter; * Mossy concrete tunnels that appear out of place in most levels may lead to the caverns of '''The Perennial Marshes;''' * Skipping a stone across a murky body of water may cause the water to become a passage to '''The Perennial Marshes''' if one submerges themselves completely; * Unknown methods in any level from [[Level 298]] to [[Level 798]] will send one to this level, and; * Any natural species that is at threat of extinction has a small chance of being automatically noclipped into this level. This is believed to function equally in [[the Frontrooms]], albeit exceedingly unlikely.
'''EXITS'''
* Portions of underground caverns may transition into a fleshy texture; following these paths will lead to [[The Hive]]; * If one enters '''The Perennial Marshes''' via a murky body of water or concrete tunnel as aforementioned, it will function inversely in most cases, and; * Staring into a refracted puddle until your vision becomes hazed may noclip one to [[Level 500]].
